description: Context complexity level definitions used by context-scaffold for recommendation and generation.

minimal.criteria: Solo project, single language, straightforward structure. One deployment target, no auth layer, no multi-service architecture.

full_single_file.criteria: Multi-layer app (frontend + backend), auth system, database, API with conventions worth documenting. Multiple backing services. Still a single deployable unit. Also appropriate when meaningful trust surfaces exist but architectural depth does not warrant a context/ split.
trigger_signals: Multiple layers (frontend + backend, or API + workers)

cascading.criteria: Multiple projects or workspaces, team environment, complex architecture with distinct subsystems. Or a single project large enough that a single file would exceed ~150 lines.
trigger_signals: Context budget pressured by loading entire file
Root file carries volatile migration notes or sprint logs
